relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH gives the final raising command.
On the first day, the dwelling is to rise.
The ark is placed and veiled.
Table and lampstand take their positions.
Incense altar and entrance screen are set.
The burnt altar stands before the entrance.
The basin is set with water.
The court and gate are raised.
Oil sanctifies the dwelling and all within it.
The altar is anointed as most holy.
The basin and stand are sanctified.
Aaron and sons are washed at the entrance.
Aaron is clothed and anointed for service.
His sons are clothed.
Their anointing carries priesthood through generations.
Moses does all YHWH commanded.
In the second year, first month, the dwelling rises.
Moses sets bases, frames, bars, and pillars.
Tent and covering are spread above.
The testimony enters the ark beneath the cover.
The ark is brought in and veiled.
The table is set outside the veil.
Bread is arranged before YHWH.
The lampstand stands opposite the table.
The lamps rise before YHWH.
The gold altar stands before the veil.
Fragrant incense rises.
The entrance screen is set.
Offerings begin at the altar.
Water waits in the basin.
Moses, Aaron, and sons wash hands and feet.
They wash whenever they approach.
The court rises, and Moses completes the work.
Cloud covers the tent, and YHWH's glory fills the dwelling.
Even Moses cannot enter because glory fills it.
When cloud lifts, Israel journeys.
When cloud stays, Israel waits.
Cloud by day and fire by night guide all Israel's journeys.

contemplative_note: Exodus 40 completes the liberation arc: the dwelling rises, glory fills it, and cloud-fire leads the freed people through every journey.
